rulings.Case Title: SHEREEN PINTO et al VS. M. GAEHWILER CONSTRUCTION,INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Barnecut Defts.' Motion To Stay The Trial Pending Resolution Of Plaintiffs' Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 22, 2015, Line 4, Barnecut Defts.' Motion To Stay The Trial Pending Resolution Of Plaintiffs' Appeal Denied. Defendants have not established that the appeal of the order granting summary judgment as to one Defendant sufficiently affects the other issues to be determined at trial to warrant a stay of the entire action. Further, Plaintiff will be unduly prejudiced if a stay is imposed. rulings.Case Title: EUGENE FURSOV VS. KIRILL SHABELSKY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Matters Admitted Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Defendant Shabelskys Further Responses To Requests For Admissions, Set No. One, To Shabelsky, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday May 22, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF EUGENE FURSOV'S Motion To Deem Matters Admitted Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Defendant Shabelskys Further Responses To Requests For Admissions, Set No. One, To Shabelsky, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Defendant Shabelsky shall serve the original verification by May 27, 2015. The general objections are stricken. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WILDA L WHITE VS. GIRISH SUBRAMANYAN MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Directing Compliance With Record Subpoena To Samuel Jinich, Ph.D.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday May 22, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT GIRISH SUBRAMANYAN, SAN FRANCISCO PSYCHIATRISTS, INC.'S Motion For Order Directing Compliance With Record Subpoena To Samuel Jinich, Ph.D. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. The parties shall meet and confer regarding a protective order and submit it to the court by June 8, 2015. If they are unable to submit a stipulated protective order to the court by that date, then they shall each submit their own protective order by that date along with a letter explaining why their order should be signed and the other side's shouldn't be signed. Once a protective order is signed and filed, defendants shall serve it as well as the order granting this motion on Dr. Jinich by hand-delivery to his office, and will file a prof of such service with the court. Within twenty days after service of the orders on Dr. Jinich, he shall produce the documents to Quest Discovery Services. Quest Discovery Services and plaintiff shall then comply with the first look procedure set forth in Exhibit E of attorney Landess' declaration for production of the records to defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON KEYES VS. PROVIDENCE FOUNDATION OF SAN FRAANCISCO, A CALIFOR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Providence Foundation Of San Francisco'S Demurrer (Control Date)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 22, 2015, Line 10, Defendant Providence Foundation Of San Francisco'S Demurrer Sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice ("RJN") is granted. Plaintiff previously filed a small claims action against Defendant seeking damages because she "was wrongfully terminated in retaliation." (RJN, Ex. A.) Plaintiff subsequently dismissed the small claims action with prejudice. (RJN, Ex. B.) "[F]or purposes of applying the doctrine of res judicata, a dismissal with prejudice is the equivalent of a final judgment on the merits, barring the entire cause of action." (Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788, 793.) A final small claims judgment is entitled to res judicata effect. (Pitzen v. Sup. Ct. (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1374.) The cases cited by Plaintiff in opposition are inapposite, as they discuss the doctrine of collateral estoppel, not res judicata. All of the claims in this action seek redress for the same harm alleged in the small claims action - Plaintiff's termination. Per the primary rights doctrine, all of the causes of action are barred by res judicata. (See Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788, 798-99.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: MAHER SOLIMAN VS. MONICA VOGEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Specially Appearing Movant Monica Pearson To Quash Service On 1) Ground Of Lack Of In Personam Jurisdiction And 2) For Failure To Abide By Code Of Civil Procedure S415.20(B) For Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 22, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT MONICA PEARSON's Motion Of Specially Appearing Movant Monica Pearson To Quash Service On 1) Ground Of Lack Of In Personam Jurisdiction And 2) For Failure To Abide By Code Of Civil Procedure S415.20(B) For Summons And Complaint Defendant's motion to quash service is denied. A nonresident defendant may be subject to jurisdiction on claims related to its activities in California. Such "limited" or "specific" personal jurisdiction requires a showing that: (1) defendant purposefully established contacts with the forum state; (2) plaintiff's cause of action "arises out of" or is "related to " defendant's contacts with the forum state; and (3) The forum's exercise of personal jurisdiction in the particular case comports with "fair play and substantial justice." [Burger King Corp. v. Rudzewicz (1985) 471 US 462, 477-478, 105 S.Ct. 2174, 2184-2185; see Data Disc, Inc. v. Systems Technology Associates, Inc. (9th Cir. 1977) 557 F2d 1280, 1287; and Vons Cos., Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc. (1996) 14 C4th 434, 446, 58 CR2d 899, 906.] Here, Defendant has such contacts with California, and Plaintiff's cause of action arises out of Defendant's contacts. Thus, California may exercise personal jurisdiction over Defendant. Plaintiff must show valid service within the statutory period. [Dill v. Berquist Const. Co., Inc. (1994) 24 CA4th 1426, 1439.] Plaintiff must demonstrate by a preponderance of the evidence that all jurisdictional criteria are met. [Ziller Electronics Lab GmbH v. Sup.Ct. (Grosh Scenic Studios) (1988) 206 CA3d 1222, 1232.] Here, Plaintiff has presented an affidavit providing that Defendant was served with summons within the statutory period. Thus Plaintiff has met his burden. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg
